steps.title: Apply the sphere volume formula with the given radius
steps.content:
The volume of a sphere is $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$. With radius $r = \frac{8}{3}$ feet, substitute directly:
$$V = \frac{4}{3}\pi \left(\frac{8}{3}\right)^3$$

steps.title: Cube the radius and simplify
steps.content:
Calculate $\left(\frac{8}{3}\right)^3 = \frac{8^3}{3^3} = \frac{512}{27}$.

Then multiply:
$$V = \frac{4}{3} \cdot \frac{512}{27}\pi = \frac{4 \cdot 512}{3 \cdot 27}\pi = \frac{2048}{81}\pi$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
